channel.title: Comments for page "Tyrone Joins the Family"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Tyrone Joins the Family"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure I've ever seen a tale of Cousin Johnny before.. But I'm excited to know that one exists now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Phenomenal work here. Your portrayal of Tyron feels immediately sympathetic and trustworthy, which makes it all the worse when things start getting creepy and wrong. The scariest part of this for me was the anticlimax—and normally that wouldn't be considered a compliment, but in this cast, it's a credit to the amount of tension the tale was able to build. Pretty much flawless as a standalone tale, though I'm curious to see where things will go next if you advance to the next round.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really great - love the tone, the use of both characters, everything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God. Absolutely chilling. Cousin Johnny scares the bejeezus out of me. I've said it before and I'll say it again — love you and your writing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is good, and you use both my character and yours very well. Looks like you may be the winner, since you've surpassed my tale in a matter of hours, so congrats.

I will upvote this - after this round of the contest is over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Beautiful work. I am really getting a sense of who Tyrone is. Somehow I'm thinking good things are not in his immediate future.

Cicadas and bees; two insects I'll never see quite the same way again, thanks to the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamn fantastic. Excellent tone, really felt the character, really pulled me in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somehow I found the last line to be the scariest part of this. Like the narrator knows that something is going to happen to them soon no matter what they do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good job on this one, Kinch. Tyrone's fear and disgust feel real, and I like the implications this sets up for Cousin Johny, having him follow and toy with Tyrone. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a lot of fun to write!
